displayTitle: Not a Defect Yet: The Three-Act Read

beats.narration: Under ultraviolet light, a line of particles glows. That is an indication. Not a defect, not yet. Reading it is Level Two work.

payload.title: NOT A DEFECT. YET.
payload.sub: What happens next is the real Level II work.

beats.narration: Three acts, in order: find the indication, interpret what caused it, evaluate only the relevant ones against acceptance criteria.
The interpreting instrument is you. Entering the booth, give your eyes at least a minute to adapt. Photochromic lenses are banned: they darken under ultraviolet.

payload.heading: Your eyes need a minute in the dark

beats.narration: The light is metered, never guessed. Ultraviolet: 1,000 microwatts per square centimeter at the surface. Ambient white: two footcandles max. Visible particles: one hundred footcandles.
So run the acts in order: find, interpret, evaluate. Adapted eyes, metered light, and criteria from the code. Never your gut.

payload.sub: Adapted eyes, metered light, criteria from the code.
